summary.intent: Fix PR review comments on the copilot-enterprise branch after merging main into it.
summary.outcome: Addressed two reviewer comments in github_copilot.rs, fixed residual merge issues where new methods from main still referenced the old `get_valid_copilot_token` instead of `get_copilot_auth`, and pushed the fixes with all 31 tests passing.

repo: Copilot tests are behind a feature flag and must be run with the feature enabled (e.g. `--features github-copilot` or similar) to execute.
The branch introduced `get_copilot_auth()` returning an auth struct with `.token` and `.base_url`, replacing the old `get_valid_copilot_token()` which only returned a token string.
New methods merged from main hardcoded `COPILOT_API_BASE` — the enterprise branch uses `auth.base_url` instead to support enterprise endpoints.

code.finding: Trailing-buffer match arm was silently dropping SSE events — flagged by reviewer as a bug.
Reviewer requested a `// NOTE:` prefix style on an existing comment.

workflow: After merging main into a feature branch, methods introduced in main must be audited for compatibility with the feature branch's refactored APIs — the compiler will catch missing methods but not subtly wrong API usage like hardcoded base URLs.

friction: Tests were silently skipped without the feature flag, making it appear the test suite passed before the flag was discovered.
The merge from main introduced new methods that still used the pre-refactor API (`get_valid_copilot_token`), requiring a second round of fixes beyond the original PR comments.
